text.content:
GM Utility Macros

Two macros ship in the Blooming Rot 2 — Utility Macros compendium. Both are GM-only and persist state in world settings, so data survives between sessions.

BR2 — Phase Clock Tracker

Tracks the two campaign-level clocks that run continuously across all five surface phases:

  • Haskur Trail Clock (0–6) — how close the trail to Haskur Vandrell is to going cold. Advances when the party loses a lead, a witness is silenced before being reached, or the party spends a full phase without forward motion toward the primary target.
  • Greyhawk Heat Clock (0–6) — how much attention the Aerdy commercial-court network is paying to the party’s movements. Advances when the party acts publicly in ways the network can observe.

Open the macro, adjust the numbers, click Save. A whispered chat summary appears with current values. Use Show only to report without changing anything.

These clocks are separate from the Phase 2 pressure clock (the six-segment Loftwick investigation clock), which is tracked manually. See Phase 2 — Quick Reference (GM) for the full pressure clock table and advancement triggers.

BR2 — Campaign State Snapshot

Tracks the carry-forward state of four major threads and five active lies. These variables are read by Phase 6 to determine which ending configuration the party reaches.

Thread fields

FieldWhat it tracksDefault
SerethThe fate of Sereth Vaun and the civil-court case. Path A = publicly indicted via the Audit Commission; Path B = quietly removed before trial; Path C = kinetically removed (killed or disappeared) by party action or conspiracy response.Unresolved
Closure motionThe Council of Barons motion to close the Audit Hall’s Phase 2 inquiry. Passed = shut down; Held over = Caelith bought time; Defeated = motion failed outright.Unresolved
PellThe fate of the conspiracy’s inside contact in the Audit Hall. Exposed = unmasked publicly; Turned = flipped as an asset; Fed false information = actively disinformed; Shadowed = party watching without tipping hand; Removed = extracted from position; Killed.Unresolved
Vesten QuillThe outcome of the false-manuscript thread into the Aerdy network. Captured = party has Quill in hand; Escaped = Quill got away; Never reached = party didn’t pursue this thread.Unresolved

Active lies

Check a lie when the party has deliberately and successfully planted it with the conspiracy’s intelligence layer — not merely said it aloud, but ensured it reached the network. Each active lie shifts the conspiracy’s response in Phase 5 and 6.

#LieEffect if active in Phase 6
1Haskur in Rel AstraNetwork pulls Greyhawk assets east; party has an easier approach window.
2Party pursuing wood correspondentNetwork watches lumber-trade routes, not the commercial-court filing route.
3Party has no usable Sereth evidenceNetwork does not activate the document-destruction protocol in Phase 6.
4Party intends to expose Galenix NaelaxNetwork moves to protect Naelax, pulling coverage away from Haskur’s operation.
5Party returning to HardbyGreyhawk tail is stood down; party enters the city unwatched.

When to update

Update at the end of any session where a thread resolves or materially changes. Run Save + Report to save and whisper a summary to GM chat.

BR2 — Phase 2 Pressure Clock

Tracks the six-segment Loftwick investigation pressure clock for Phase 2. This is separate from the two campaign-level clocks in the Phase Clock Tracker.

Running the macro opens a small dialog showing the current clock image, the segment counter, and the telegraph label for the current state. Two buttons — Retreat and Advance — step the clock down or up. The dialog also places a transparent clock tile in the top-right corner of the active scene and updates it in real time whenever you click a button.

Telegraph states

SegmentsLabelWhat it means at the table
0CLEARNo heat on the party’s Loftwick movement. Begin here.
1TAMPERINGSomeone was looking. The records alcove was accessed after the party left, or a contact is asking uncomfortable questions.
2WARNINGTrina won’t budge. A witness has been approached by the other side. The party’s margin of movement is narrowing.
3RUMOURThe party is recognisable in the city. Asking questions is starting to produce blowback rather than answers.
4WITNESS LOSSCut the thread that hurts most. A key witness becomes unavailable, recants, or goes silent. GM’s choice which thread.
5MOTION FILED48 hours remain. The closure motion is on the floor. The party can still act, but every action now has a time cost.
6DOOR SHUTSGM’s choice — the worst outcome that fits. The commission inquiry closes, a critical piece of evidence is destroyed, or a key NPC departs. Something irreversible happens.

Advancement triggers

Advance the clock by one segment whenever:

  • The party makes a significant public mistake (caught searching, discovered in a restricted area, wrong person contacted).
  • A named NPC makes a report to the opposing interest.
  • The party misses a scripted pressure event without any mitigation.
  • The party delays significantly between scenes (a full in-game day of inaction).

Retreat the clock by one segment when the party successfully suppresses a threat, turns an NPC witness, or plants a lie that diverts the opposing interest (see Active Lies in the Campaign State Snapshot).

The tile

The macro automatically places a transparent clock image in the top-right corner of the active scene and updates it whenever the state changes. Use Remove tile from current scene in the dialog if you need to clear it (e.g. when switching to a scene where showing the clock would be a spoiler).

Welcome to Blooming Rot, Part 2

A nine-phase Greyhawk adventure for D&D 2024, set in the pre-Wars Yeomanry, Hardby, Rel Astra, Greyhawk City, and the deep roads beneath the Cairn Hills. For 4–6 PCs, levels 7–11. Approximately 18–25 sessions if every sandbox branch is engaged, or 12–15 sessions on the main thread.

The investigation in one paragraph

Caelith Dunivar, the Yeomanry's quiet spymaster, has buried a body in his own city's strong-room: forty pages of foreign correspondence, a dead man's ledger, a sealed packet that smells of an ink no Yeomanry house uses. He summons the party, hands them the case, and asks them to follow it. Across six surface phases — Act I — they trace a paper trail through Loftwick, Hardby, Rel Astra, and Greyhawk City, until at the Brass Crow they hold the man at the end of the trail — only to learn the man is a door, the door is rented, and the path beyond it descends. Act II follows that road down, in three phases beneath the Cairn Hills.

How this module is organised

Nine phases in two acts. Act I — the Paper (Phases 1–6) is the surface investigation. Act II — the Route (Phases 7–9) is the descent. A tenth phase is declared but not yet built.

Compendium packContents
Phases 1–9 — JournalsThe narrative content, phase by phase: read-aloud blocks, GM notes, scene-by-scene structure. Phases 1–6 are the surface investigation; Phases 7–9 are the descent.
Phases 1–9 — ActorsNPC stat blocks for every named character. Most use MM 2024 archetypes; the major figures (Caelith, Trina, Sereth, etc.) have bespoke stat blocks.
Phases 1–6 — ScenesPre-built Foundry Scenes with battlemaps loaded. Phases 7–9 ship keyed maps in assets/maps/ to drop in as needed.
Sandbox — Journals + ActorsPer-PC anchor NPCs and downtime references for Loftwick, Hardby, Rel Astra, and Greyhawk City. Use between phases or whenever the party has a free day.
Connective layerFour packs that run alongside the whole campaign: Per-PC Plot Threads, The Aerdy Commercial-Court Network, World Stakes (emotional-cost vignettes), and Relief Scenes (tonal counterweight).

How to read the GM-facing materials

  • Bordered cream-tinted blockquotes are read-aloud / read-to-the-table text. You can read them verbatim or paraphrase.
  • Prose paragraphs are GM-facing explanation, mechanics, and judgment calls.
  • Tables are reference data — carry-forward variables, NPC quick reads, clock advancement triggers.
  • The phase chapters in docs/blooming-rot-2-gm-prep.pdf reproduce all of this in a single ~760-page printable document, with flow diagrams, NPC profile cards, and inline illustrations — designed for session-by-session prep ahead of game day.

Foundry-specific tips

  • Compendium imports: drag any actor or journal into the world to use it. NPCs come with full stat blocks, items linked to PHB/DMG/MM 2024, and round metal-ringed tokens already wired.
  • Items are pre-linked to the official D&D 2024 modules where the party will recognise them. If you have Player's Handbook, Dungeon Master's Guide, and Monster Manual installed, weapons render with proper masteries (Vex, Sap, Nick, etc.) automatically.
  • Maps: keyed location maps for every important interior live in the module's assets/maps/ folder. Drop them into a Scene at 5 ft per square.
  • Handouts: Phase 6's Ashen Itinerary ships in three fidelity states (intact, partially burned, scorched fragments) so the GM can match the climax outcome. Other handouts (registry pages, ledger excerpts, road notes, manuscript analyses) live in each phase's Player Handouts journal.

Reading order for the GM

  1. Read this Welcome.
  2. Read Phase 1 — Return to Loftwick in full. The first three sessions are tightly scripted; everything afterward grows from how the players handle them.
  3. Read Phase 2 — Departure and the Sandbox Reference for the city the party is in.
  4. Before each session, re-read the phase chapter for that session's expected scene. Each phase chapter has a flow diagram showing where decision points branch.
  5. Trust the carry-forward tables. Later phases read what the party did earlier (Sereth's path, the Closure motion, Pell's fate, Vesten Quill's outcome, the Permission Door method, and so on).

If something breaks

If the party misses a clue, ignores a node, or fail-forwards the climax, the campaign continues. Every phase has at least one fallback path; even total tactical failure in the Stonecistern leaves the route mark on the wall.

If a party member captures, kills, or fails to catch Haskur Vandrell, the campaign continues. He is not authorially protected.

If a Foundry import looks broken, reach out: github.com/morrigangirl/blooming-rot-2/issues.

What's beyond

Phases 7–9 (the descent) are included. Phase 10 is declared but not yet built: at the Lower Route Fork the under-road splits into the Recovery Road (where names are burned), the Witness Road, and the Black Ledger Road.

The campaign should not name the Underdark, Eclavdra, House Eilserv, the Vault, the Demonweb, or Lolth in player-facing text until Phase 8 at the earliest. Restraint at the table makes the threshold land.

Credits

Adventure design and writing: Aoibh Wood. Mechanical assist (D&D 2024 PHB/DMG/MM linkage, NPC archetypes): the official 2024 books, with thanks to Wizards of the Coast.

Begin in Phase 1. Caelith is waiting.
